title: leucocraspedum montanum
intro: Leucocraspedum montanum, also known as the Mountain Craspedia or Mountain Spray, is a perennial plant species in the daisy family, native to Australia and New Zealand. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which can be attributed to its high content of sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, relieving digestive issues, and exhibiting antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of Leucocraspedum montanum can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to support immune function and alleviate respiratory issues.
